description: graph-metadata.json manual.related_to omits deep-ai-council despite 8 production require() calls in deep-ai-council/scripts/orchestrate-{topic,session}.cjs consuming deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/* modules (all 5 council primitives consumed). This breaks bi-directional graph traversal: a search for 'what consumes deep-loop-runtime' via graph-metadata.related_to misses the canonical council consumer. The depends_on edge from deep-ai-council → deep-loop-runtime exists implicitly via require statements but is invisible to the graph-metadata-driven discovery layer until manual.related_to is patched. Note: devin §C originally said '5 require() statements in test files'; the orchestrator-side bundle gate corrected this to 8 production require() calls in production .cjs.
recommended_patch: Add 'deep-ai-council' to manual.related_to array. Result: ['deep-review', 'deep-research', 'system-code-graph', 'deep-ai-council']. Edges block (manual section L43-52) plus derived edges should follow automatically via generate-context.js refresh.
